task_completion.description: Add Jetpack Navigation KTX dependencies to the app-level build.gradle.kts file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'build.gradle.kts' (Module: app) file is open in the editor, showing 'implementation' lines for 'androidx.navigation:navigation-fragment-ktx' and 'androidx.navigation:navigation-ui-ktx' (version 2.7.7 or compatible).

task_completion.description: Create the Home, Dashboard, and Settings Fragments along with their corresponding XML layout files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Android Studio Project tool window shows 'HomeFragment.kt', 'DashboardFragment.kt', and 'SettingsFragment.kt' under 'java/com.example.navapp', AND 'fragment_home.xml', 'fragment_dashboard.xml', and 'fragment_settings.xml' under 'res/layout'.

task_completion.description: Create the navigation graph and bottom navigation menu XML files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Project tool window or editor tabs show 'nav_graph.xml' located in 'res/navigation' and 'bottom_nav_menu.xml' located in 'res/menu'.

task_completion.description: Update activity_main.xml to include a FragmentContainerView and a BottomNavigationView.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'activity_main.xml' file is open in the editor (Code or Design view) showing both a '<androidx.fragment.app.FragmentContainerView>' (or '<fragment>') and a '<com.google.android.material.bottomnavigation.BottomNavigationView>'.

task_completion.description: Update MainActivity.kt to link the BottomNavigationView with the NavController.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'MainActivity.kt' file is open in the editor showing code that sets up the navigation controller with the bottom navigation bar (e.g., calling 'setupWithNavController').

task_completion.description: Ensure the project builds successfully and/or runs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Android Studio 'Build' tool window displays a 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message, OR the Android Emulator is visible running the 'NavigationApp' with a visible bottom navigation bar containing Home, Dashboard, and Settings tabs.

integrity.description: The agent used Android Studio to implement the changes rather than exclusively using terminal commands to echo text into files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Android Studio's UI being actively used (e.g., editor tabs open, project tool window expanded, build processes running in the IDE).

integrity.description: The modifications and builds are performed on the correct target project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Android Studio window title or project tree root clearly shows 'NavigationApp', and the package name 'com.example.navapp' is visible in the code or project structure.

integrity.description: The agent actually implemented the required XML and Kotlin logic rather than just creating empty files to pass file-existence checks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Open editor tabs for the XML layouts, nav graph, and Kotlin classes show actual implementation code (e.g., XML tags, class definitions, navigation setup logic) rather than being blank.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at '/home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/NavigationApp' with the package 'com.example.navapp'. Required dependencies are 'androidx.navigation:navigation-fragment-ktx' and 'androidx.navigation:navigation-ui-ktx' (version 2.7.7 or later). The implementation requires 3 Fragment classes (HomeFragment.kt, DashboardFragment.kt, SettingsFragment.kt), 1 nav graph (nav_graph.xml), 1 menu (bottom_nav_menu.xml), AND 3 corresponding layout files (fragment_home.xml, fragment_dashboard.xml, fragment_settings.xml). The main activity layout must contain both a FragmentContainerView and a BottomNavigationView.
